quote: Sloth, like rust, consumes faster than labor wears, while the key often used is always bright.
author: Franklin

quote: Sloth, if it has prevented many crimes, has also smothered many virtues.
author: Colton

quote: Flee sloth, for the indolence of the soul is the decay of the body.
author: Cato

quote: Sloth makes all things difficult, but industry all easy; and he that riseth late must trot all day, and shall scarce overtake his business at night; while laziness travels so slowly that poverty soon overtakes him.
author: Franklin

quote: Sloth is torpidity of the mental faculties; the sluggard is a living insensible.
author: Zimmermann

quote: Sloth never arrived at the attainment of a good wish.
author: Cervantes

quote: A smile is the whisper of a laugh.
author: Child's Definition

quote: A face that cannot smile is never good.
author: Martial

quote: Smiles from reason flow, to brute denied, and are of love the food.
author: Milton

quote: A woman has two smiles that an angel might envy — the smile that accepts a lover before words are uttered, and the smile that lights on the first-born babe, and assures it of a mother's love.
author: Haliburton

quote: A smile is the color which love wears, and cheerfulness, and joy — these three. It is the light in the window of the face, by which the heart signifies to father, husband, or friend, that it is at home and waiting.
author: H. W. Beecher

quote: A beautiful smile is to the female countenance what the sunbeam is to the landscape: it embellishes an inferior face, and redeems an ugly one.
author: Lavater

quote: Something of a person's character may be discovered by observing how he smiles. Some people never smile; they only grin.
author: Bovee

quote: Laughter is day, and sobriety is night; a smile is the twilight that hovers gently between both, more bewitching than either.
author: H. W. Beecher

quote: A face that cannot smile is like a bud that cannot blossom which dries up on the stalk.
author: H. W. Beecher

quote: The habit of sneering marks the egotist, the fool, or the knave, or all three.
author: Lavater

quote: The most insignificant people are the most apt to sneer at others. They are safe from reprisals, and have no hope of rising in their own esteem but by lowering their neighbors.
author: Hazlitt

quote: A sneer is often the sign of heartless malignity.
author: Lavater

quote: He who forgets his own friends meanly to follow after those of a higher degree is a snob.
author: Thackeray

quote: A snob is one who is always pretending to be something better — especially richer or more fashionable than others.
author: Thackeray

quote: Modesty and humility are the sobriety of the mind; temperance and chastity are the sobriety of the body.
author: Winchcote

quote: Man is a social animal, formed to please and enjoy in society.
author: Montesquieu

quote: Man, in society, is like a flower blown in its native bud. It is there only that his faculties, expanded in full bloom, shine out, there only reach their proper use.
author: Cowper

quote: We take our colors, chameleon-like, from each other.
author: Chamfort

quote: There are four varieties in society; the lovers, the ambitious, observers, and fools. The fools are the happiest.
author: Taine

quote: Society is composed of two great classes: those who have more dinners than appetite, and those who have more appetite than dinners.
author: Chamfort

quote: Society is now one polished horde, formed of two mighty tribes, the bores and bored.
author: Byron

quote: We are more sociable, and get on better with people by the heart than the intellect.
author: Bruyere

quote: Society is like a lawn, where every roughness is smoothed, every bramble eradicated, and where the eye is delighted by the smiling verdure of a velvet surface.
author: Washington Irving

quote: Society is the atmosphere of souls; and we necessarily imbibe from it something which is either infectious or healthful.
author: Bp. Hall

quote: Society has only one law, and that is custom. Even religion is socially powerful only so far as it has custom on its side.
author: Hamerton

quote: Society is no comfort to one not sociable.
author: Shakespeare

quote: The secret of success in society is a certain heartiness and sympathy.
author: Emerson

quote: It is in the middle classes of society that all the finest feeling, and the most amiable propensities of our nature do principally flourish and abound.
author: Colton

quote: A soldier seeking the bubble reputation even in the cannon's mouth.
author: Shakespeare

quote: Soldiers are the only carnivorous animals that live in a gregarious state.
author: Zimmermann

quote: Ignorance, poverty, and vanity make many soldiers.
author: Zimmermann

quote: A wise man is never less alone than when he is alone.
author: Swift

quote: If the mind loves solitude, it has thereby acquired a loftier character, and it becomes still more noble when the taste is indulged in.
author: W. Humboldt

quote: Conversation enriches the understanding, but solitude is the school of genius.
author: Gibbon

quote: Solitude is not measured by the miles of space that intervene between a man and his fellows.
author: Thoreau

quote: If from society we learn to live, it is solitude should teach us how to die.
author: Byron

quote: Solitude is the audience chamber of God.
author: L. E. Landon

quote: Those are never alone who are accompanied by noble thoughts.
author: Sir P. Sidney

quote: Solitude shows us what we should be; society shows us what we are.
author: Cecil

quote: I love to be alone. I never found the companion that was so companionable as solitude.
author: Thoreau

quote: Solitude, the safeguard of mediocrity, is to genius the stern friend, the cold, obscure shelter where moult the wings which will bear it farther than suns and stars.
author: Emerson

quote: Solitude cherishes great virtues and destroys little ones.
author: Sydney Smith

quote: In solitude, where we are least alone.
author: Byron

quote: A song will outlive all sermons in the memory.
author: H. Giles

quote: The age of chivalry is gone; that of sophisters, economists, and calculators has succeeded.
author: Burke

quote: Some men weave their sophistry till their own reason is entangled.
author: Johnson

quote: On the sands of life sorrow treads heavily, and leaves a print time cannot wash away.
author: H. Necle

quote: Sorrow is a kind of rust of the soul which every new idea contributes in its passage to scour away.
author: Johnson

quote: The deeper the sorrow the less tongue it has.
author: Talmud

quote: Sorrows are our best educators. A man can see further through a tear than a telescope.
author: Unknown

quote: Earth hath no sorrow that heaven cannot heal.
author: Moore

quote: Give sorrow words: the grief, that does not speak, whispers the o'erfraught heart, and bids it break.
author: Shakespeare

quote: Sorrows remembered sweeten present joy.
author: Pollok

quote: Our sorrows are like thunder-clouds, which seem black in the distance, but grow lighter as they approach.
author: Richter

quote: Out of suffering have emerged the strongest souls; the most massive characters are seamed with scars.
author: E. H. Chapin

quote: We tell our triumphs to the crowd, but our own hearts are the sole confidants of our sorrows.
author: Bulwer

quote: Social sorrow loses half its pain.
author: Johnson

quote: Take all sorrow out of life, and you take away all richness, and depth, and tenderness.
author: Unknown

quote: Sorrow is Mount Sinai, if one will but go up and talk with God face to face.
author: H. W. Beecher

quote: Whatever that be which thinks, which understands, which wills, which acts, it is something celestial and divine, and on that account must necessarily be eternal.
author: Cicero

quote: The problem of restoring to the world original and eternal beauty is solved by the redemption of the soul.
author: Emerson

quote: The wealth of a soul is measured by how much it can feel; its poverty by how little.
author: W. R. Alger

quote: I am fully convinced that the soul is indestructible, and that its activity will continue through eternity.
author: Goethe

quote: Life is the soul's nursery — its training place for the destinies of eternity.
author: Thackeray

quote: Style may be defined, 'proper words in proper places.'
author: Swift

quote: Style is the dress of thoughts.
author: Chesterfield

quote: Obscurity in writing is commonly a proof of darkness in the mind; the greatest learning is to be seen in the greatest plainness.
author: Wilkins

quote: Generally speaking, an author's style is a faithful copy of his mind.
author: Goethe

quote: Style is the gossamer on which the seeds of truth float through the world.
author: Bancroft

quote: Style is a man's own; it is a part of his nature.
author: Buffon

quote: Intense study of the Bible will keep any man from being vulgar in point of style.
author: Coleridge

quote: Clear writers, like clear fountains, do not seem so deep as they are; the turbid looks most profound.
author: Landor

quote: When we meet with a natural style we are surprised and delighted, for we expected to find an author, and have found a man.
author: Pascal

quote: Sublimity is Hebrew by birth.
author: Coleridge

quote: The sublime and the ridiculous are often so nearly related that it is difficult to class them separately.
author: Thomas Paine

quote: One source of sublimity is infinity.
author: Burke

quote: From the sublime to the ridiculous there is but one step.
author: Napoleon

quote: The truly sublime is always easy, and always natural.
author: Burke

quote: Success in life is a matter not so much of talent or opportunity as of concentration and perseverance.
author: C. W. Wendte

quote: The road to success is not to be run upon by seven-leagued boots. Step by step, little by little, bit by bit — that is the way to wealth, that is the way to wisdom, that is the way to glory.
author: Charles Buxton

quote: Success is full of promise till men get it, and then it is as a last year's nest, from which the bird has flown.
author: H. W. Beecher

quote: The greatest results in life are usually attained by simple means and the exercise of ordinary qualities.
author: Feltham

quote: The surest way not to fail is to determine to succeed.
author: Sheridan

quote: Nothing succeeds so well as success.
author: Talleyrand

quote: The talent of success is nothing more than doing what you can do well, and doing well whatever you do without a thought of fame.
author: Longfellow

quote: A little tact and wise management may often evade resistance, and carry a point, where direct force might be in vain.
author: Anon

quote: Tact comes as much from goodness of heart as from fineness of taste.
author: Endymion

quote: Talent is power; tact is skill.
author: Unknown

quote: Talent is wealth; tact is ready money.
author: W. P. Scargill

quote: Talent is something, but tact is everything.
author: W. P. Scargill

quote: Men of talent are men for occasions.
author: Unknown

quote: Whatever you are from nature, keep to it; never desert your own line of talent.
author: Sydney Smith

quote: Talent for talent's sake is a bauble and a show. Talent working with joy in the cause of universal truth lifts the possessor to new power as a benefactor.
author: Emerson

quote: There is a sacredness in tears. They are not the mark of weakness, but of power.
author: Washington Irving

quote: Tears are the safety-valves of the heart when too much pressure is laid on it.
author: Albert Smith

quote: Love is loveliest when embalmed in tears.
author: Walter Scott

quote: Tears are often the telescope through which men see far into heaven.
author: H. W. Beecher

quote: Good temper, like a sunny day, sheds a brightness over everything; it is the sweetener of toil and the soother of disquietude.
author: Washington Irving

quote: The happiness and misery of men depend no less on temper than fortune.
author: Rochefoucauld

quote: A tart temper never mellows with age; and a sharp tongue is the only edged tool that grows keener with constant use.
author: Washington Irving

quote: If religion does nothing for your temper it has done nothing for your soul.
author: Clayton

quote: Bad temper is its own scourge. Few things are more bitter than to feel bitter.
author: Charles Buxton

quote: Temperance is the lawful gratification of a natural and healthy appetite.
author: J. B. Gough

quote: Temperance puts wood on the fire, meal in the barrel, flour in the tub, money in the purse, credit in the country, contentment in the house, clothes on the children, vigor in the body, intelligence in the brain, and spirit in the whole constitution.
author: Franklin

quote: Temperance and labor are the two best physicians; the one sharpens the appetite — the other prevents indulgence to excess.
author: Rousseau

quote: Temptations are a file which rub off much of the rust of our self-confidence.
author: Fenelon

quote: It is one thing to be tempted, another thing to fall.
author: Shakespeare

quote: God is better served in resisting a temptation to evil than in many formal prayers.
author: Penn

quote: Every moment of resistance to temptation is a victory.
author: Faber

quote: To realize God's presence is the one sovereign remedy against temptation.
author: Fenelon

quote: The absence of temptation is the absence of virtue.
author: Goethe

quote: There is no love-broker in the world can more prevail in man's commendation with woman, than report of valor.
author: Shakespeare

quote: The time for reasoning is before we have approached near enough to the forbidden fruit to look at it and admire.
author: Margaret Percival

quote: To be trusted is a greater compliment than to be loved.
author: J. Macdonald

quote: The man who trusts men will make fewer mistakes than he who distrusts them.
author: Cavour

quote: Truth is the foundation of all knowledge and the cement of all societies.
author: Dryden

quote: Every violation of truth is a stab at the health of human society.
author: Emerson

quote: Truth lies in character. Christ did not simply speak the truth; he was truth.
author: Robertson

quote: Truth and love are two of the most powerful things in the world; and when they both go together they cannot easily be withstood.
author: Cudworth

quote: The greatest homage we can pay to truth is to use it.
author: Emerson

quote: Truth can hardly be expected to adapt herself to the crooked policy and wily sinuosities of worldly affairs; for truth, like light, travels only in straight lines.
author: Colton

quote: Truth is always consistent with itself, and needs nothing to help it out.
author: Tillotson

quote: The greatest friend of truth is time; her greatest enemy is prejudice; and her constant companion is humility.
author: Colton

quote: Truth crushed to earth will rise again; the eternal years of God are hers.
author: Bryant

quote: Peace if possible, but truth at any rate.
author: Luther

quote: To thine own self be true, and it must follow, as the night the day, thou canst not then be false to any man.
author: Shakespeare

quote: Tyranny and anarchy are never far asunder.
author: J. Bentham

quote: Bad laws are the worst sort of tyranny.
author: Burke

quote: Tyranny is always weakness.
author: J. R. Lowell

quote: Where law ends, tyranny begins.
author: William Pitt, the Elder

quote: Rebellion to tyrants is obedience to God.
author: Franklin

quote: Necessity is the argument of tyrants; it is the creed of slaves.
author: William Pitt, the Younger

quote: All unbelief is the belief of a lie.
author: H. Bonar

quote: In all unbelief there are these two things: a good opinion of one's self, and a bad opinion of God.
author: H. Bonar

quote: There is no strength in unbelief. Even the unbelief of what is false is no source of might.
author: G. Macdonald

quote: Nothing in this world is so good as usefulness. It binds your fellow creatures to you, and you to them.
author: B. C. Brodie

quote: Vanity is the poison of agreeableness.
author: Greville

quote: The most violent passions have their intermissions; vanity alone gives us no respite.
author: Rochefoucauld

quote: It is our own vanity that makes the vanity of others intolerable to us.
author: Rochefoucauld

quote: Of all our infirmities, vanity is the dearest to us; a man will starve his other vices to keep that alive.
author: Franklin

quote: Vanity is the quicksand of reason.
author: George Sand

quote: Vanity is the fruit of ignorance.
author: Ross

quote: Variety is the very spice of life, that gives it all its flavor.
author: Cowper

quote: Vice stings us even in our pleasures, but virtue consoles us even in our pains.
author: Colton

quote: Vice is a monster of so frightful mien as to be hated needs but to be seen; yet seen too oft, familiar with her face, we first endure, then pity, then embrace.
author: Pope

quote: No man ever arrived suddenly at the summit of vice.
author: Juvenal

quote: Vice loses half its evil by losing all its grossness.
author: Burke

quote: Victories that are easy are cheap. Those only are worth having which come as the result of hard fighting.
author: H. W. Beecher

quote: Pursue not a victory too far. He hath conquered well that hath made his enemy fly.
author: Herbert

quote: Who overcomes by force hath overcome but half his foe.
author: Milton

quote: To be innocent is to be not guilty; but to be virtuous is to overcome our evil feelings and intentions.
author: Penn

quote: That virtue which requires to be ever guarded is scarce worth the sentinel.
author: Goldsmith

quote: Virtue is its own reward, and brings with it the truest and highest pleasure.
author: Newman

quote: Virtue is an angel; but she is a blind one and must ask of knowledge to show her the pathway that leads to her goal.
author: Horace Mann

quote: Virtue is the first title of nobility.
author: Moliere

quote: Perfect virtue is to do unwitnessed what we should be capable of doing before all the world.
author: Rochefoucauld

quote: It has ever been my experience that folks who have no vices have very few virtues.
author: Lincoln

quote: A good wife is heaven's last, best gift to man — his gem of many virtues, his casket of jewels.
author: Jeremy Taylor

quote: No man knows what the wife of his bosom is — what a ministering angel she is, until he has gone with her through the fiery trials of this world.
author: Washington Irving

quote: Great souls have wills; feeble ones have only wishes.
author: Chinese Proverb

quote: He who has a firm will molds the world to himself.
author: Goethe

quote: People do not lack strength; they lack will.
author: Victor Hugo

quote: There never was a good war, or a bad peace.
author: Franklin

quote: War is the business of barbarians.
author: Napoleon

quote: To be prepared for war is one of the most effectual ways of preserving peace.
author: Washington

quote: Common-sense in an uncommon degree is what the world calls wisdom.
author: Coleridge

quote: Wisdom is the right use of knowledge. To know is not to be wise.
author: Spurgeon

quote: Wisdom is to the mind what health is to the body.
author: Rochefoucauld

quote: The wisest man is generally he who thinks himself the least so.
author: Boileau

quote: It is more easy to be wise for others than for ourselves.
author: Rochefoucauld

quote: True wisdom is to know what is best worth knowing, and to do what is best worth doing.
author: Humphrey

quote: Wonder is involuntary praise.
author: Young

quote: The man who cannot wonder, who does not habitually wonder and worship, is but a pair of spectacles behind which there is no eye.
author: Carlyle

quote: Words are both better and worse than thoughts; they express them, and add to them; they give them power for good or evil.
author: Tryon Edwards

quote: Words are like leaves; and where they most abound, much fruit of sense beneath is rarely found.
author: Pope

quote: Words are things; and a small drop of ink, falling like dew upon a thought, produces that which makes thousands, perhaps millions, think.
author: Byron

quote: Zeal is very blind, or badly regulated, when it encroaches upon the rights of others.
author: Quesnel

quote: Zeal without knowledge is like expedition to a man in the dark.
author: John Newton

quote: Experience shows that success is due less to ability than to zeal. The winner is he who gives himself to his work, body and soul.
author: Charles Buxton

quote: Next to God we are indebted to women, first for life itself, and then for making it worth having.
author: Bovee

quote: There is a woman at the beginning of all great things.
author: Lamartine

quote: Women are the poetry of the world in the same sense as the stars are the poetry of heaven.
author: Hargrave

quote: Woman was taken out of man; not out of his head to top him, nor out of his feet to be trampled underfoot; but out of his side to be equal to him, under his arm to be protected, and near his heart to be loved.
author: M. Henry

quote: In the end, thought rules the world.
author: J. McCosh

quote: Thinking is the talking of the soul with itself.
author: Plato

quote: All grand thoughts come from the heart.
author: Vauvenargues

quote: Spiritual force is stronger than material; thoughts rule the world.
author: Emerson

quote: The happiness of your life depends upon the quality of your thoughts.
author: Marcus Antoninus

quote: Thought is the seed of action.
author: Emerson

quote: Thought means life, since those who do not think do not live in any high or real sense. Thinking makes the man.
author: A. B. Alcott

quote: The more we study the more we discover our ignorance.
author: Shelley

quote: Studies serve for delight, for ornament, and for ability.
author: Bacon

quote: Learning without thought is labor lost; thought without learning is perilous.
author: Confucius
